name: Pinseque and Imperial Canal of Aragón Loop
name_original: Ermita de Peraman y Murallas de Grisén — circular por el canal Imperial de Aragón
description: Hike a moderate 10.8-mile (17.4 km) circular route along the Imperial Canal of Aragón, featuring historic bridges and the Hermitage of the V
region_intro: Hiking around Grisén offers accessible routes characterized by its riverside landscapes and notable historical engineering. The terrain is primarily flat, following the course of the Jalón River and the impressive Canal Imperial de Aragón. This area provides a gentler outdoor experience, blending natural serenity with historical structures like the "Walls of Grisén" aqueduct. Paths often traverse riverside woods, offering tranquil natural surroundings for relaxed walks. Best hiking trails around Grisén The most popular hiking route is Pinseque and Imperial Canal of Aragón Loop, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the significant 18th-century Canal Imperial de Aragón. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alagón loop from Alagón, a moderate 9.5 miles (15.4 km) path. This route offers varied scenery, often following the river or canal. Local hikers also love the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Wave loop from Grisén, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Grisén is defined by the confluence of the Jalón River and Canal Imperial de Aragón, riverside woods, and the historic aqueduct. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Grisén are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 5 reviews. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Grisén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Grisén?
A: Hiking around Grisén primarily features flat terrain, following the courses of the Jalón River and the impressive Canal Imperial de Aragón. You'll find pleasant excursions along riverside woods, offering tranquil natural surroundings for relaxed walks.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Grisén?
A: Yes, Grisén offers several easy routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14941740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Wave loop from Grisén&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) trail, typically completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40364423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jubo Bridge loop from Pinseque&lt;/a&gt;, which is also around 6.9 km.

Q: What are the main natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Grisén?
A: Grisén's hiking routes highlight the confluence of the Jalón River and the Canal Imperial de Aragón, particularly at a spot known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6950938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Caracol (The Snail)&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the historic aqueduct, popularly known as the "Walls of Grisén," which carries the Canal over the Jalón River. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Jalón River&lt;/a&gt; and various bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1355893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavería Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Grisén area?
A: Yes, many routes around Grisén are circular. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976091521/pinseque-and-imperial-canal-of-aragon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinseque and Imperial Canal of Aragón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.8 miles (17.4 km). For an easier circular walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14941740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Wave loop from Grisén&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Grisén a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Grisén is well-suited for family-friendly hikes due to its generally flat and accessible terrain. The routes along the Jalón River and the Canal Imperial de Aragón offer gentle outdoor experiences, perfect for relaxed walks with children. The historical engineering marvels also provide interesting points of discovery.

Q: What is the average rating for hiking trails in Grisén?
A: The hiking trails in Grisén are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil riverside paths and the unique historical structures.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While Grisén is known for its gentler trails, there are moderate options for those seeking a longer walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24313012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alagón loop from Alagón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.5-mile (15.4 km) path that offers varied scenery along the river or canal, providing a good distance challenge.

Q: What historical engineering marvels can be seen on the trails?
A: The most prominent historical engineering marvel is the 18th-century Canal Imperial de Aragón. Hikers can witness its impressive structure, including the aqueduct known as the "Walls of Grisén," which carries the Canal over the Jalón River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1355893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavería Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is another notable structure along the Canal.

Q: Are there any lakes or water bodies to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, in addition to the Jalón River and the Canal Imperial de Aragón, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5238060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Larralde&lt;/a&gt;, a lake that offers another natural water feature to explore near the hiking trails in the Grisén area.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Grisén?
A: There are 8 hiking routes available in Grisén, offering a variety of options from easy strolls to moderate excursions. This includes 3 easy and 5 moderate trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Grisén?
A: Grisén offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the riverside paths and historical sites. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended during those months.
tips:
The Hermitage of the Virgen de la Ola is a Marian sanctuary located in Peramán, an old hamlet in the municipality of Bárboles in the valley of the Jalón River. Built in the 17th century, it has a rectangular floor plan.
Its exterior has been renovated with exposed brick and has a small tower without bells.
It is located on the banks of the Jalón River and is a place of Marian devotion.
Historic arch bridge built in the 18th century to cross the Imperial Canal of Aragon. Nice picture to photograph!

The Jubo Bridge has a fascinating history spanning from the 18th century to the 21st century.
Originally, its construction began in the 18th century, but due to the War of Independence, it remained unfinished for many years.
Finally, in more recent times, it was completed with a wooden and steel walkway.
This bridge is a pedestrian crossing over the Imperial Canal of Aragon, located near the town of Pinseque and a few meters from the Santa Emilia beacon.

faqs.question: Is this a circular hiking route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning you will end your hike at the same point where you started. It offers a varied experience without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of riverside paths along the Jalón River and canal-side trails following the historic Imperial Canal of Aragón. You'll also encounter several historical bridges, providing a mix of natural and engineered landscapes.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the 17th-century Hermitage of the Virgin of the Wave, located by the Jalón River. The route also features impressive historical bridges such as the Clavería Bridge over the Imperial Canal of Aragon and the Jubo Bridge, both offering great photographic opportunities and a glimpse into the region's past.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as a circular route starting and ending near Grisén, you would typically look for parking within or on the outskirts of the village. Public parking options are usually available in small towns like Grisén.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate. While it's suitable for most hikers with a reasonable level of fitness, its length of nearly 17.4 km and duration of over 4 hours mean it's not ideal for absolute beginners. However, with minimal elevation gain, it's a good option for those looking to step up from easier walks.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the location, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot in Aragón, so early mornings or late afternoons would be preferable if hiking during that season. Winters are usually mild but can be damp.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails along canals and rivers in rural areas are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near historical sites, agricultural land, or if you encounter other hikers or wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this route along the Imperial Canal of Aragón or to visit the historical sites mentioned. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The route passes near Grisén, a village where you may find basic amenities such as local cafes, bars, or small shops. For more extensive options regarding restaurants or accommodation, you might need to venture to larger nearby towns.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Canal Imperial Aragon, Murallas de Grisen, Camino de Barboles, Camino de Peramán, and even directly crossing the Puente Jubo, which is also a named trail segment.
